subject: english
topic_title: Plural Forms of Nouns, Stress of Five Syllable Words, and Creative Writing
learning_objectives: Apply Plural Forms of Nouns correctly in sentences.
Identify and pronounce Stress of Five Syllable Words correctly.
Write a clear Creative Writing; Features of a Short Story using the expected features and structure.
Summarize the key ideas in Agriculture.

sections.heading: Structure: Plural Forms of Nouns
sections.content: English nouns derive their plural forms in a variety of ways: Addition of –s (tables), Addition of –es (boxes), Addition of –en/-ren (children), Changing 'f' to '-ves' (thieves), Changing 'y' to 'ies' (cities), Changing vowels (feet, mice). Unchanged species: deer, cattle, sheep, furniture, information, luggage. Nouns ending in 's' treated as singular: news, Physics, Economics.

sections.heading: Speech Work – Stress of Five Syllable Words
sections.content: Words ending in –ion, have the second syllable from the end stressed (examiNAtion). Stress the third syllable from the end if a word ends in –ity (authenTIcity) or –al (agriCULtural). Words ending in –ism, -ive, -ible/-able have stress on the fourth syllable from the back (imPErialism, repreSENtative).

sections.heading: Writing: Creative Writing; Features of a Short Story
sections.content: Creative writing involves skillful and imaginative production of something original. Features: Theme (central point), Plot (story line), Characters (names and dialogue), Setting (where and when). Must have a beginning, a climax, an anti-climax and a conclusion. Language: past tense (except dialogue).

sections.heading: Summary Writing – Agriculture
sections.content: A summit to tackle the problems of food security in Africa was organized with delegates from different African countries. NEPAD adviser Professor Richard Mkandawire hoped for technical assistance programmes within African countries.
